spec_match.rationale: While it writes to the real Cache API if available, the Service Worker registration and the offline navigation flow are entirely simulated on the client side.

interactive.rationale: Provides a great three-panel walkthrough with clear interactive states and detailed step-by-step logs of the prefetch, offline toggle, and navigation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the blurb perfectly, illustrating prefetch caching and offline navigation.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers cache hit vs cache miss when offline mode is simulated without prefetching.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus link in the see also section points to ID 5122321072758784 rather than the principal parent ID 4996410105397248 (ServiceWorker support for Speculation Rules Prefetch).

design_system.rationale: Nicely styled and behaves well across different display sizes, using the standard style variables.

openQuestions.title: Correct ChromeStatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The ChromeStatus link in the see also section points to ID 5122321072758784 rather than the principal parent ID 4996410105397248 (ServiceWorker support for Speculation Rules Prefetch). Fix this reference.

openQuestions.title: Register actual Service Worker for offline navigation
openQuestions.detail: Currently, the offline navigation and service worker log are simulated. To make it genuinely interactive and spec-compliant, we should register a real local Service Worker that intercepts the navigation to /v138/serviceworker-support-for-speculation-rules-prefetch/ and responds with the cached page from Cache API, allowing a true offline transition in-page or in an iframe.

summary: A beautifully formatted and highly logical step-by-step simulation of offline speculation prefetch caching, with a real Cache API hook but simulated SW routing and a ChromeStatus link mismatch.
